subject: History
chapter_name: Socialism in Europe and the Russian Revolution
overall_learning_objectives: Distinguish between Liberals, Radicals, and Conservatives
Understand the core ideas of Socialism and Karl Marx's vision
Identify the causes and sequence of the 1917 Russian Revolutions
Learn about the role of Vladimir Lenin and the Bolsheviks
Identify the impact of the revolution on workers and peasants
Understand Stalin’s collectivization program at a basic level

lesson_plan.title: The Age of Social Change
teaching_strategy: Discussion on how factories changed people's lives
student_activities: Create a table matching political groups to their beliefs
List problems faced by workers during early industrialization
assessment: Oral quiz: Who supported the right of women to vote?

lesson_plan.title: The Russian Empire & Economy
topics_covered: The Russian Empire in 1914 and Tsar Nicholas II
Socialism in Russia: Formation of political parties
teaching_strategy: Map pointing: The vastness of the Russian Empire
Visual analysis: Pictures of workers in factories
student_activities: Identify the main religions and regions of the Russian Empire
Compare Russian peasants with European peasants
assessment: Short answer: Why were Russian peasants unique?

lesson_plan.title: 1905 Revolution: A Turbulent Time
topics_covered: The impact of the First World War on Russia
teaching_strategy: Narrative description of the 'Bloody Sunday' procession
Flowchart: Causes and effects of the 1905 unrest
student_activities: Write a headline for a newspaper about Bloody Sunday
Discuss why the Tsar dismissed the first Duma

lesson_plan.title: The February Revolution
topics_covered: The fall of monarchy in Petrograd
Strikes and the role of women workers
teaching_strategy: Comparison: The layout of Petrograd (Right vs. Left bank)
student_activities: Draw a timeline of the February Revolution
Role-play: Workers demanding bread and peace
assessment: Paragraph writing: What led the Tsar to abdicate?

lesson_plan.title: The October Revolution
topics_covered: Conflict between the Provisional Government and Bolsheviks
teaching_strategy: Discussion on Lenin's three main demands
Visual mapping of the October events in Petrograd
student_activities: List the three points of the 'April Theses'
Create a 'Bolshevik' poster for the 1917 revolution
assessment: Oral quiz on the October Revolution leader
Peer discussion on the April Theses

lesson_plan.title: What Changed after October?
topics_covered: Bolshevik changes: Nationalization of banks and land
The Civil War: Reds, Whites, and Greens
teaching_strategy: T-chart: Changes in cities vs. changes in the countryside
student_activities: Color-code the groups in the Civil War on a worksheet
List the new rules for clothing (the 'Budenovka' hat)
assessment: Short written test on Bolshevik changes

lesson_plan.title: Stalin and the Global Legacy
topics_covered: The global influence of the Russian Revolution
student_activities: Identify countries influenced by Socialism on a map
Complete a chapter summary mind map

teaching_aids: Map of the Russian Empire in 1914
teacher_tip: Focus on the 'Before and After' aspect—how Russia looked under the Tsar versus how it changed under the Bolsheviks—to help students grasp the scale of the revolution easily.
